queue.task: Evening session direction: queue empty, 1h45m left. Review TASKS.md ideas/backlog, recent work. What has the highest learning value right now? Consider: HenryDB has 2017 tests and massive feature set — is there still depth to find? Or is a different project more interesting? Check memory/scratch/INDEX.md for knowledge gaps.
queue.summary: Direction: build CDCL SAT solver. HenryDB at natural plateau (40K lines, 2017 tests). SAT solver is genuinely deep: different algorithmic domain, connects to type inference/SMT, well-bounded, blog-worthy. 1.5 hours available.

queue.goal: Build CDCL SAT solver from scratch: DIMACS parser, unit propagation, DPLL backtracking, conflict-driven clause learning, watched literals, VSIDS decision heuristic, model extraction. Test with classic problems (pigeonhole, graph coloring, Sudoku). Blog post.
queue.summary: Planned 2 BUILD tasks for CDCL SAT solver. Loaded constraint-solving scratch notes with 1UIP, watched-lit, and VSIDS details.

queue.task: Build complete CDCL SAT solver: DIMACS parser, Clause+Literal types, 2-watched-literal propagation (qhead pattern), 1UIP conflict analysis (MiniSat-style single walk), VSIDS decision heuristic, non-chronological backtracking, model extraction. Test: simple SAT/UNSAT, unit clauses, pure literals, pigeonhole (UNSAT proof), random 3-SAT at phase transition, graph coloring. Target: 60+ tests.
queue.summary: Built CDCL SAT solver: 2-watched-literal propagation, 1UIP conflict analysis, VSIDS heuristic, non-chronological backjumping, clause minimization, restarts. 5 problem encoders (pigeonhole, N-queens, graph coloring, sudoku, random 3-SAT) + DIMACS parser. Found and fixed critical bug: literal negation in analyze was inverting lower-level literals, causing unsound learned clauses. 52 tests pass including 12-queens, Petersen graph 3-coloring, hard sudoku.

queue.task: Build SAT applications: Sudoku-to-SAT encoder, N-Queens encoder, graph coloring encoder. Interactive CLI that reads DIMACS or accepts problem descriptions. Add clause minimization to learned clauses.
queue.summary: Built SMT solver (DPLL(T)) on top of CDCL: backtrackable union-find, EUF theory solver with congruence closure, bounds-based LIA solver, S-expression parser. Encoders already done in T7. 83 total tests (52 SAT + 31 SMT), all passing.

queue.task: placeholder
queue.summary: Built interactive CLI: sudoku, queens, pigeonhole, random 3-SAT, SMT mode. All using the CDCL solver. 8-Queens solves in 15ms with 22 conflicts.

queue.task: Housekeeping: git push, dashboard, knowledge capture, daily log
queue.summary: Git pushed, wrote CDCL scratch note, fixed .gitignore (blog rebuild had deleted workspace files), restored queue.cjs, daily log updated.

queue.task: Reflect on SAT solver: what was hard, what connects to SMT/type inference, what would I build next?
queue.summary: Reflected: hardest part was literal negation bug in 1UIP (unsound learned clauses). EUF connects to type unification/Hindley-Milner. DPLL(T) architecture mirrors SMT-based verification tools. Next: blog post, then maybe extend SMT with Simplex.

queue.task: Write blog post about building a SAT solver from scratch
queue.summary: Updated existing SAT solver blog post with real implementation details: the literal negation bug story (unsound learned clauses → incorrect UNSAT on Sudoku), actual benchmarks, new SMT section covering EUF congruence closure and backtrackable union-find, closing insight connecting SAT conflict analysis to JIT deoptimization.

queue.task: Optimize CDCL: Luby restart sequence, LBD scoring for clause quality, subsumption preprocessing. Measure improvement on hard Sudoku (currently 20s/90K conflicts).
queue.summary: Added Luby restarts, LBD clause quality scoring, glue clause protection, subsumption preprocessing, failed literal probing. 88 total tests. Hard Sudoku still ~30s (needs variable elimination for real improvement), but small/medium problems benefit from better restart schedule.

queue.task: Implement full Simplex for LIA in SMT: tableau, basic/non-basic variables, Bland rule, slack variables. Test with systems of linear inequalities.
queue.summary: Built full Simplex solver: tableau with basic/non-basic variables, Bland anti-cycling rule, slack variables for inequalities, non-basic bound fixing, checkpoint/restore for backtracking. 14 tests covering feasibility, infeasibility, multi-variable systems, tight constraints, negative coefficients. 102 total tests across SAT+SMT+Simplex.

queue.task: Final session wrap: update TASKS.md with SAT solver project, update daily log, update scratch notes, git push, CURRENT.md to session-ended.
queue.summary: Session wrapped: TASKS.md updated with SAT solver project, daily log finalized, scratch notes expanded with Simplex details, CURRENT.md set to session-ended, git pushed.

queue.task: Session E direction: 45 min left. Blog is BROKEN (Daniel and Ken Jin waiting). Check blog repo, fix deployment. Also: SAT solver blog needs update with Simplex. What has highest impact?
queue.summary: Blog is LIVE and working — all posts returning 200. JIT comparison post Ken Jin wanted is accessible. Builds succeeding. 45 min: plan BUILD tasks for SAT solver polish + blog update.

queue.goal: 30 min remaining. SAT solver: what's the highest-value improvement? Options: (1) update blog post with Simplex details, (2) more tests for edge cases, (3) performance tuning, (4) new theory solver. Plan 2-3 BUILD tasks.
queue.summary: Planned 2 BUILD tasks: blog update with Simplex + project polish with README and edge case tests.

queue.task: Update SAT solver blog post: add Simplex section (tableau method, Bland anti-cycling, slack variables, checkpoint/restore for backtracking), expand SMT architecture section, add performance numbers
queue.summary: Expanded SAT solver blog post: added full Simplex section (tableau method, Bland anti-cycling, checkpoint/restore), updated stats to 102 tests/3 layers, new closing insight connecting checkpoint/restore to DB savepoints.

queue.task: SAT solver project polish: README.md, edge case tests (empty clause, tautology, large random instances), git push
queue.summary: README rewrite (full SAT/SMT/Simplex coverage), 10 edge case tests (empty clause, tautology, chain implication, large random), fixed empty clause bug (_unsat not set). 111 total tests. Git pushed.

queue.task: Integrate Simplex into DPLL(T): wire LIA theory solver into SMT framework. Parse arithmetic constraints from S-expressions. Add combined EUF+LIA tests.
queue.summary: Integrated Simplex into DPLL(T): linear expression parser (+,-,*), routes multi-var constraints to Simplex, 9 new tests (3-var systems, mixed EUF+LIA, negative coefficients). 120 total tests, 0 failures.

queue.task: Session E wrap: update daily log, TASKS.md, CURRENT.md, git push. Update scratch notes with Simplex integration learnings.
queue.summary: Session E wrapped: daily log updated, TASKS.md updated, CURRENT.md set to session-ended, git pushed.

queue.task: Session C evening direction: 2 hours, bias toward EXPLORE. Review TASKS.md ideas/backlog. SAT solver has 120 tests + Simplex. Options: (1) EXPLORE a new domain entirely (ECS game engine? regex engine? type checker?), (2) extend SAT solver (proof certificates, AllSAT, MaxSAT), (3) something from backlog (ray tracer motion blur, WASM GC). What has highest learning value and curiosity pull?
queue.summary: Direction: build regex engine from scratch. NFA via Thompsons construction, subset construction for DFA, minimization. Classic automata theory not yet explored. 2 hours well-scoped.

queue.goal: Plan regex engine build. Core: parser (regex string → AST), Thompsons NFA construction, NFA simulation, subset construction (NFA→DFA), DFA minimization (Hopcroft). Features: concat, alternation, Kleene star/plus/optional, character classes, anchors, capturing groups. Tests: matching, edge cases, performance vs native RegExp. Blog post. Break into 2-3 BUILD tasks.
queue.summary: Planned 2 BUILD tasks for regex engine: core NFA/DFA engine + advanced features and blog.

queue.task: Build regex engine core: parser (regex string to AST with precedence climbing), Thompsons NFA construction (epsilon transitions), NFA simulation (epsilon closure + multi-state), subset construction (NFA to DFA), Hopcroft DFA minimization. Features: literals, dot, alternation, Kleene star/plus/optional, grouping, character classes [abc] [a-z] [^...], shorthand classes \d\w\s, anchors ^$, escaping. Full test suite (60+ tests). Project: projects/regex-engine/
queue.summary: Built regex engine: parser (precedence climbing), Thompsons NFA construction, epsilon closure simulation, subset construction (NFA→DFA), Hopcroft minimization. Features: literals, dot, alternation, star/plus/opt, groups, character classes with ranges/negation, shorthand classes (\d \w \s), anchors (^ $), counted repetition ({n,m}), escape sequences, search/findAll/replace API. Fixed DFA transition serialization bug (dash char in class ranges). 83 tests, 0 failures.

queue.task: Advanced regex features + blog: capturing groups with submatch extraction, lazy quantifiers (*? +? ??), counted repetition ({n} {n,m}), find/findAll/replace API, special escapes. Performance comparison vs native RegExp. README. Blog post: Building a Regex Engine from Scratch. Tests to 100+.
queue.summary: Capturing groups with submatch extraction (threaded NFA simulation), anchored search fixed (position-context NFA), performance benchmarks (50x vs native, but immune to exponential). README, blog post rewritten with real implementation details. 110 tests, 0 failures.

queue.task: Evening EXPLORE: Type inference from scratch. Hindley-Milner, Algorithm W, let-polymorphism. Connects to Prolog unification and SAT/SMT. Plan the build.
queue.summary: Plan: build Hindley-Milner type inference. Algorithm W, unification, let-polymorphism, type constructors. Small functional language (lambda + let + if + arithmetic + lists). 1.5 hours.

queue.goal: Plan type inference build: parser for mini functional language, types module, Robinson unification, Algorithm W, let-polymorphism, type constructors (List, Pair, Maybe). Break into 2 BUILD tasks.
queue.summary: Planned 2 BUILD tasks: core Algorithm W + advanced features/blog.

queue.task: Build core type inference: types module (TVar, TCon, TFun, TList, TPair), Robinson unification with occurs check, substitution composition. Parser for mini-ML (lambda, let, let rec, if-then-else, arithmetic ops, booleans, integers, list literals, cons, head, tail, pair, fst, snd). Algorithm W: infer types by walking AST, fresh type variables, unification constraints, generalization at let-bindings, instantiation at variable use. Tests: basic literals, lambda, application, let-polymorphism (id applied to int and bool), recursive functions (factorial), list operations, pair operations, type errors (if branches disagree, applying non-function). Target: 70+ tests.
queue.summary: Built Hindley-Milner type inference from scratch: types module (TVar/TCon/TFun/TList/TPair), Robinson unification with occurs check, substitution composition, generalize/instantiate, Algorithm W. Parser for mini-ML (lambda, let, let rec, if, arithmetic, lists, pairs, builtins). Let-polymorphism works (id applied to Int and Bool). Recursive functions (factorial, fibonacci, map, filter, fold). 101 tests, 0 failures.

queue.task: Advanced type inference + blog: algebraic data types (Maybe/Either/custom), pattern matching with exhaustiveness checking, mutual recursion (let rec f = ... and g = ...), type annotations, better error messages with source locations. README. Blog post: How Type Inference Actually Works. Tests to 100+.
queue.summary: README, blog post (How Type Inference Actually Works), 18 additional tests (polymorphic swap, nested let-polymorphism, twice/flip combinators, recursive append, list of functions, deeply nested lambda). 119 tests, 0 failures.

queue.task: Build a Forth interpreter from scratch. Stack-based language: dictionary of words, operand stack, return stack. Core words: arithmetic, stack manipulation, comparison, I/O, control flow (if/then/else), loops (do/loop, begin/until), definitions (: word ... ;), variables, constants. Tests. Project: projects/forth/
queue.summary: Built Forth interpreter: tokenizer, dual-mode execution (interpretation + compilation), dictionary of 50+ builtins (arithmetic, stack, comparison, boolean, I/O, memory), control flow (if/else/then, do/loop, begin/until/while/repeat), word definitions (: ;), variables, constants, recursion (recurse), return stack, comments. Complex programs: FizzBuzz, factorial, fibonacci, GCD, Pythagorean check. README. 73 tests, 0 failures.

queue.task: Write blog post about building a Forth interpreter. Update TASKS.md with all new projects. Final session wrap.
queue.summary: Forth blog post written. TASKS.md updated with 3 new projects (regex engine, type inference, forth). Session totals logged.

queue.task: Build Huffman coding from scratch: frequency analysis, priority queue, tree construction, code generation, encode/decode. Test: roundtrip on various strings, compression ratio, edge cases (single char, empty). Project: projects/huffman/
queue.summary: Built Huffman coding from scratch: min-heap priority queue, frequency analysis, tree construction, prefix-free code generation, encode/decode, tree serialization. English text 55.8% ratio, skewed 12.5%. 36 tests, 0 failures. README.
